channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: 'Papel de um bom juiz não é ser estrela', diz Mendonça
item.description: STF Minister André Mendonça stated that the role of a good judge is not to be a star, but to decide correctly for the right reasons. He emphasized discretion and austerity in judicial proceedings, noting his role as rapporteur in the Banco Master investigations and the INSS inquiry involving a son of President Lula. His comments come amid tensions with Alexandre de Moraes and rising political prominence ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Arthur Lira lança pré-candidatura ao Senado Federal por AL durante evento em Maceió
item.description: Federal deputy Arthur Lira (PP) launched his pre-candidacy for the Federal Senate from Alagoas during an event in Maceió, emphasizing his tenure as former Chamber president and pledging to honor his father’s legacy, former senator Benedito de Lira. He is contesting one of the state’s two Senate seats in 2026 and noted that his 18-year-old son, Álvaro Lira, is expected to run for federal deputy.

item.title: Haddad diz que Tebet tem mais raízes em SP que Tarcísio e afirma não rejeitar apoio de conservadores
item.description: Former Finance Minister Fernando Haddad (PT) defended Simone Tebet (MDB) after she was called a 'foreigner' by allies of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos), stating she has deeper roots in São Paulo than the governor. Haddad confirmed Tebet will change her electoral domicile to São Paulo and run for Senate on his ticket. He also said he does not rule out support from conservative parties, as long as principles and values are upheld, citing the decisive PP backing in his 2012 São Paulo mayoral campaign.

item.title: As ações da força-tarefa do TRE do Rio para blindar eleições do crime organizado
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Regional Electoral Court (TRE-RJ) has formed a task force to counter organized crime infiltration in the 2026 elections, including identifying and replacing polling stations in high-risk areas and sharing intelligence on candidates with possible criminal ties. Led by Judge Claudio de Mello Tavares, the initiative aims to prevent voter coercion, illegal campaign financing, and candidacies linked to criminal factions, integrating security agencies and the Regional Electoral Prosecutor’s Office.

item.title: Haddad defende Alckmin vice de Lula, reedita 2022 e diz que Tebet tem mais raiz que Tarcísio em SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad, former Finance Minister and pre-candidate for Governor of São Paulo, defended Geraldo Alckmin remaining as Lula’s vice-presidential running mate in 2026, arguing the campaign should replicate the successful 2022 strategy. He criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for lacking familiarity with the state, stating that Simone Tebet has deeper roots in São Paulo than the current governor.

item.title: 'Tarcísio não tem nenhuma familiaridade até hoje com SP', diz Haddad em 1ª entrevista como pré-candidato a governador
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) officially launched his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo in 2026, rebutting criticisms from Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos), whom he accused of having 'no familiarity whatsoever with São Paulo'. Haddad also defended Simone Tebet's (MDB) Senate bid, stating she has deeper roots in the state than Tarcísio, and emphasized his own political fighting spirit since 2016.

item.title: No STF, Zanin vota para manter privatização da Sabesp; Fux suspende julgamento
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin, the rapporteur of the case, voted to deny the motions seeking to suspend the São Paulo law authorizing the privatization of Sabesp, citing procedural grounds without addressing the merits. Justice Luiz Fux then requested a review, transferring the decision to the physical plenary with no set date. The issue had been discussed in meetings between Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and several justices on Wednesday.

item.title: Quem é Eduardo Cavaliere, advogado que assume como prefeito mais jovem da história do Rio
item.description: Eduardo Cavaliere, 31, becomes the youngest mayor in Rio de Janeiro's history after Eduardo Paes (PSD) steps down to run for state governor. A lawyer and close aide to Paes, Cavaliere previously led the Municipal Secretariat of Environment and the Chief of Staff office, and was elected state deputy in 2022 before returning to City Hall. His appointment reflects the continuity of Paes’ political project within the PSD.

item.title: Lula escala ministro para participar da coordenação de campanha
item.description: Social Development Minister Wellington Dias has been appointed to join the coordination of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's (PT) re-election campaign. He will contribute to political articulation with governors, mayors and leaders, particularly in the Northeast. Dias, a former governor of Piauí and licensed senator, will use accumulated vacation time to focus on campaign activities.

item.title: Haddad: Tarcísio não cumpriu promessa de combate à violência contra a mulher
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for failing to fulfill his promise to prioritize combating violence against women, made during a debate. Haddad was confirmed as the PT’s pre-candidate for the São Paulo governorship in 2026, as part of a strategy to strengthen opposition to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. The state is pivotal in the presidential race due to its electorate of 34.4 million voters.

item.title: Doechii curte balada em São Paulo um dia antes de seu show no Lollapalooza
item.description: Rapper Doechii was spotted enjoying a nightclub in downtown São Paulo, a day before her Lollapalooza 2026 performance. She was seen dancing and taking photos with fans at the Martinelli Building, showing warmth and connection with her audience. Doechii, a Grammy winner for Best Rap Album, is regarded as one of the leading voices in female rap in the U.S. and is set to perform at the festival this Friday.

item.title: Crusoé: Felca defende a Lei Felca (mais ou menos)
item.description: Influencer Felipe Bressanim, known as Felca, posted a video defending the 'Lei Felca' (Digital ECA), which caused online confusion and prompted the Lula government to delay signing the decree. However, he stated his support is partial or conditional, indicating he backs the law's intent but has concerns about its execution.

item.title: A decisão de Tereza Cristina sobre a chapa com Flávio Bolsonaro ao Planalto
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) declined the invitation to be the running mate on Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) presidential ticket, choosing to remain in the Senate. She was the preferred choice of PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto for vice president due to her gender, background as former Agriculture Minister, and appeal to the União-PP federation and agribusiness sector. The PL is now seeking another woman to fill the vice-presidential slot.

item.title: Meio-Dia em Brasília: o que Vorcaro vai falar na delação premiada
item.description: The Meio-Dia em Brasília program this Friday, the 20th, will cover the initial negotiations for the plea bargain of banker Daniel Vorcaro, former controller of Banco Master, and discuss the tribute made by Minister Gilmar Mendes to Alexandre de Moraes at the STF, in addition to mentioning an unspecified pre-candidacy.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula ainda tenta empurrar Alckmin para o Senado
item.description: President Lula (PT) continues to try to convince Vice President Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) to run for the Senate from São Paulo in 2026, despite saying the vice-presidential spot remains open for him. During an event at the ABC Metalworkers Union, Lula said he would be very happy to have the former São Paulo governor as a Senate candidate.

item.title: Governo edita MPs para baratear diesel e garantir piso do frete
item.description: The government issued Provisional Measures 1.344/2026 and 1.343/2026 to curb rising diesel prices and protect the national freight floor, responding to oil price volatility from the Middle East war. MP 1.344/2026 allocates up to R$10 billion in subsidies for road-use diesel until December 31, 2026, and increases export tariffs on crude oil (12%) and diesel (50%) to boost domestic supply. MP 1.343/2026 introduces penalties, including suspension of the RNTRC, for employers paying below the minimum freight rate, aiming to prevent trucker strikes.

item.title: Haddad critica Tarcísio, mira interior de SP, e despista sobre vice
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) launched his pre-candidacy for the São Paulo governorship, criticizing Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' management on education and the economy, citing a R$26 billion drop in the state’s cash reserves. He emphasized the need to improve his appeal among conservative voters in the interior and said he will finalize his ticket with allies such as Márcio França, Guilherme Boulos, and Tabata Amaral after securing Lula’s backing.

item.title: Wellington Dias vai coordenar campanha de Lula à reeleição em 2026, diz ministério
item.description: Minister of Social Development Wellington Dias (PT) will be one of the coordinators of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's 2026 re-election campaign, according to the MDS press office. He will play a strategic role in articulating with party leaders, governors, mayors, and social movements, as he did in the victorious 2022 campaign. Dias is evaluating whether to remain in the MDS, with a decision expected next week.

item.title: Moro se filia ao PL de Bolsonaro em 24 de março
item.description: Sergio Moro decided to join the Liberal Party (PL) to run for governor of Paraná in October 2026, after facing obstacles with the PP in União Brasil. The formal announcement will be made on March 24, 2026, with public support from Flávio Bolsonaro and Edson Vasconcelos as a potential running mate. Paraná Pesquisas poll shows Moro leading in all first and second-round scenarios.

item.title: Lula diz que fará documento para cobrar ações de paz dos EUA, da China e da Rússia
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) announced he will publish a document calling on the five permanent members of the UN Security Council — the U.S., China, Russia, the UK, and France — to take responsibility for global conflicts, stating the world is experiencing its highest level of armed conflict since World War II. He criticized these nations for spending $2.7 trillion on weapons last year while under-investing in food, education, and humanitarian aid. Lula also confirmed his pre-candidacy for president in 2026, framing the fight against far-right extremism as both a domestic and international struggle.

item.title: Lei amplia garantias para financiamentos do Pronaf
item.description: President Lula signed Law 13.356/2026, authorizing the Fund Guaranteeing Operations (FGO) to back up to R$500 million in Pronaf credit operations, lowering barriers to rural credit access for family farmers. The measure abolishes the pecuniary commission and aims to ease loans for production costs, equipment, and property investment, particularly for those lacking traditional collateral.

item.title: Buscava ideia de desenvolver plano de desenvolvimento para o País, mas vim para SP, diz Haddad
item.description: Former Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, the PT's pre-candidate for São Paulo governor, said he had the idea of developing a national development plan but ended up working in São Paulo.

item.title: Vamos fazer debate leve, mas pautado por ideias e críticas construtivas, diz Haddad
item.description: Fernando Haddad, former Finance Minister and PT’s pre-candidate for São Paulo governor, advocates for a light yet idea-driven electoral debate with constructive criticism, centered on the state’s needs.

item.title: Haddad diz que conversas sobre o vice começam nos próximos dias
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) stated that discussions to define his running mate for the São Paulo gubernatorial race will begin in the coming days, involving figures such as Márcio França, Caio França, Tabata Amaral, and Guilherme Boulos. He expressed preference for Geraldo Alckmin as vice president, though talks have not yet started, and emphasized his campaign will focus on policy debates and countering misinformation, aiming to position São Paulo as a showcase for impactful public policies.

item.title: Lei reduz alíquotas de tributos para indústria química
item.description: President Lula signed Law 228/2026, which reduces PIS and Cofins tax rates for the chemical and petrochemical industries from March to December 2026 as a transitional measure ahead of the 2027 tax reform. The law sets a R$2 billion cap on fiscal renunciation and forecasts R$1.1 billion in additional tax credits, easing prior requirements for performance goals, beneficiary counts, and oversight that had been vetoed earlier.

item.title: TSE tem 2 votos para cassar deputado do PL em Sergipe por fraude à cota de gênero
item.description: Two TSE ministers voted to cancel the mandate of Deputy Ícaro de Valmir (PL-SE) over gender quota fraud in the PL’s 2022 election slate. The trial was suspended by a request for review, with no date set for resumption. If confirmed, the seat would likely go to André David, a Civil Police delegate from Aracaju.

item.title: Preço do diesel no Brasil sobe e chega a R$ 7,22, diz pesquisa
item.description: The average diesel price in Brazil reached R$ 7,22 per liter on March 19, 2026, rising R$ 1,48 since February 28, according to TruckPag data from over 143,000 transactions at 4,664 stations. The increase is sharper than ANP’s weekly reports due to data collection delays, and persists despite the government’s R$ 30 billion package to mitigate impacts, which includes PIS/Cofins exemption and subsidies. The surge is driven by international oil prices, as about 30% of Brazil’s diesel is imported and follows global market trends.

item.title: Durigan assume Fazenda e herda pressão por gastos em ano eleitoral de Lula
item.description: Dario Durigan assumed the role of Minister of Finance on Friday, February 20, replacing Fernando Haddad, who left the position to run in the 2026 elections. Durigan, previously the executive secretary of the ministry, inherits pressure to control public spending in an election year marked by Lula’s re-election bid. His appointment was formalized in the Official Gazette of the Union.

item.title: Ex-agentes processam diretor do FBI e alegam que foram demitidos por investigação sobre Trump
item.description: Two former FBI agents are suing FBI Director Kash Patel, claiming they were summarily fired for their involvement in the 'Arctic Frost' investigation into Donald Trump's efforts to overturn the 2020 election results. They allege their terminations violated constitutional rights to due process and free speech, with no prior investigation, notice, or opportunity to appeal. The lawsuit also names Attorney General Pam Bondi, the FBI, and the Department of Justice as defendants.

item.title: Delação de Vorcaro cria guerra de versões e pode mudar rumo da eleição
item.description: The potential plea bargain of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Master, has sparked a clash between Lula and Bolsonaro supporters, each side trying to blame the other. A confidentiality agreement between Vorcaro, the PF, and the PGR has already been signed, and political leaders say no branch of government will be spared, though the electoral impact depends on revelations about bank fraud and political bribery.

item.title: Eduardo Paes deixa Prefeitura do Rio para disputar governo estadual
item.description: Eduardo Paes (PSD) is leaving the Rio de Janeiro City Hall this Friday, the 20th, to run for a third time for state governor. His formal departure will take place at 4 PM at the City Palace in Botafogo, and his deputy, Eduardo Cavaliere, 31, will assume the role, becoming the youngest mayor in Rio's history. Paes leads polls with 46% voter intent, followed by Douglas Ruas (PL) at 13%, while Ítalo Marsili (Novo) and Wilson Witzel (DC) tie for third at 5% each.

item.title: ECA Digital: fiscalização efetiva de aferição de idade começa em 2027
item.description: The ANPD has released a timeline for implementing age verification on social media, with effective enforcement scheduled only for 2027. The measure aims to protect children and adolescents online while balancing security with privacy and personal data protection. Starting this month, the ANPD will publish initial parameters for reliable age verification mechanisms, focusing first on transparency and guidance before applying sanctions.

item.title: Paris, Marselha, Le Havre: eleições locais na França antecipam disputa presidencial
item.description: The municipal elections in Paris, Marseille, and Le Havre, held this Sunday (22), are testing the strength of the right and far-right in France after 25 years of left-wing dominance in Paris and may determine whether the far-right can govern Marseille. The runoff results will significantly impact the 2027 presidential race, serving as a barometer of political forces ahead of the national election.

item.title: Lula vai à Colômbia para cúpula da Celac e fórum com países africanos
item.description: President Lula attends the 10th Celac Summit and the 1st Celac-Africa High-Level Forum in Bogotá, Colombia, focusing on regional integration, poverty reduction, food security, climate change, and cooperation with the Global South. The agenda includes meetings with leaders such as Gustavo Petro (Colombia) and Yamandú Orsi (Uruguay), as well as African representatives, to strengthen partnerships between Latin America, the Caribbean, and Africa.

item.title: Paes deixa prefeitura para disputar governo do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Eduardo Paes (PSD) resigns as mayor of Rio de Janeiro to run for state governor in 2026, handing over the role to his deputy, Eduardo Cavaliere. He previously served as mayor for four terms since 2021 and ran for governor in 2006 and 2018, losing the runoff to Wilson Witzel in 2018.

item.title: Greca deixa o PSD
item.description: Former Curitiba mayor Rafael Greca announced yesterday his departure from the Social Democratic Party (PSD) and declared his support for Ratinho Júnior's presidential candidacy. Greca signed his membership form for the MDB, with the party president's backing. The move signals a political realignment in Paraná ahead of the 2026 presidential race.

item.title: Dario Durigan é nomeado como novo ministro da Fazenda no lugar de Haddad
item.description: Dario Durigan was appointed as the new Minister of Finance, replacing Fernando Haddad, who left the post to run for governor of São Paulo. Durigan, previously the ministry's executive secretary, has experience in economic policy, tax reform, and dialogue with productive sectors, having worked on Haddad’s team in São Paulo City Hall and in the private sector, including WhatsApp. Lula confirmed the appointment at a São Paulo event, praising Durigan’s discretion, articulation skills, and readiness to manage economic challenges during the 2026 presidential re-election campaign.

item.title: Castro exonera 11 secretários em meio a possível renúncia ao governo do RJ
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) dismissed 11 state secretaries amid speculation of a possible resignation to avoid having his mandate annulled by the TSE. The dismissals, published in the State Official Gazette, also included subsecretaries and positions in coordinations and directorates, seen as preparation for his potential departure. Castro faces charges of political and economic abuse of power related to the Ceperj/Uerj case in the 2022 elections, and if convicted, would become ineligible for office.

item.title: Uma língua e uma toga podem influenciar os rumos da eleição
item.description: The 2026 election gained two new protagonists: Daniel Vorcaro’s testimony, as a whistleblower whose revelations could destroy reputations, and André Mendonça’s robe, as a STF judge set to rule on those disclosures. Their actions may directly shape the campaign’s trajectory, given the potential of Vorcaro’s information to derail political ambitions. The scenario points to high tension between plea bargains and the judiciary, with significant electoral consequences.

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei que obriga Hino Nacional nas escolas
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law maintaining the weekly requirement to sing the Brazilian National Anthem in public and private schools in São Paulo, while removing the obligation to hoist the National Flag weekly. The updated regulation, effective immediately, allows schools to choose the day for the anthem, preferably Fridays, and retains the rule to sing it on the weekday before September 7.

item.title: Gilmar homenageia Serra: “Poucos se dedicaram tanto ao Brasil”
item.description: Gilmar Mendes paid tribute to José Serra, highlighting his long dedication to Brazil, including his work as Health Minister where he led generic drug policies and HIV combat programs, and his proposal for periodic review of public spending. Serra, a former governor and senator, has been withdrawn from public life since his 2021 Parkinson’s diagnosis, although he attempted a political comeback in 2022 without electoral success.

item.title: PT privilegia disputa ao Congresso na distribuição de fundo eleitoral e joga governos estaduais para o fim da fila
item.description: The PT has prioritized Senate and Chamber of Deputies elections in its 2026 electoral fund distribution, placing gubernatorial candidates fourth, behind only state legislative races. The shift, approved in an internal resolution at the end of 2025 and to be ratified in April, reflects the party’s concern over the renewal of two-thirds of the Senate and the threat of a bolsonarist-dominated Senate, given that senators vote on STF minister impeachments. President Lula reportedly said he prefers winning one Senate election over five gubernatorial races.

item.title: Crusoé: 79% querem que Lula declare PCC e CV como terroristas
item.description: A RealTime BigData poll for Crusoé shows 79% of Brazilians support classifying PCC and CV as terrorist organizations by the Lula government, while 21% oppose it. Despite federal resistance, public opinion strongly favors the designation.

item.title: Ozempic no SUS e sem patente: como canetas emagrecedoras já mudaram o mundo — e o que vem por aí
item.description: Rio de Janeiro became the first Brazilian city to offer Ozempic through the public health system (SUS) following the expiration of semaglutide's patent, opening the door for generic versions and potential price reductions. While Mayor Eduardo Paes symbolically administered the first dose, President Lula hesitated to adopt the drug nationally, emphasizing lifestyle changes and cautioning against viewing obesity as a moral failing, even as medical experts argue it must be treated as a chronic disease.

item.title: Carta ao Leitor: Agenda estrelada
item.description: VEJA launched its 2026 coverage calendar with a nearly five-hour live Oscar broadcast hosted by Raquel Carneiro and streamed via VEJA+ across multiple platforms. The outlet is also preparing coverage for the VEJA Infrastructure Forum and the Brazil Insights Forum in New York, as well as its first World Cup coverage via VEJA+, led by Fábio Altman and Marcos Uchôa. VEJA+, operating for less than a year, already reaches 8 million monthly users.

item.title: Jogos de hoje, sexta, 20 de março: onde assistir ao vivo e horários
item.description: On Friday, March 20, 2026, football matches will take place across European leagues, the Argentine Championship, and the Brazilian Women's Serie A. Key fixtures include Cagliari vs Napoli (Serie A, 2:30 PM), Bournemouth vs Manchester United (Premier League, 5:00 PM), and Flamengo vs Cruzeiro (Brazilian Women's Serie A, 9:00 PM), broadcast on ESPN, Disney+, Sportv, and CazéTV.

item.title: Com Haddad fora, Durigan assume a Fazenda com desafio de evitar novas turbulências
item.description: Fernando Haddad is leaving the Ministry of Finance to run for governor of São Paulo, complying with electoral law that requires public officials to step down six months before elections. His successor, Dario Durigan, the current executive secretary of the Finance Ministry, takes office amid heightened fiscal fragility, including primary and nominal deficits, rising debt, and slowing economic activity, inheriting a situation that risks worsening in the election year. While Durigan’s appointment signals continuity and is backed by his experience in Haddad’s team, analysts warn the lack of room for policy shifts may hinder fiscal progress, increasing fears of further deterioration in public accounts.

item.title: Governo Lula avalia novos decretos para regulamentar big techs
item.description: The Lula government is evaluating new decrees to regulate big techs, relying on the STF's decision on the Marco Civil da Internet and existing congressional bills, without needing new legislation. Measures include specific protections against digital violence toward women and implementation of the ECA Digital, requiring age verification on platforms. The strategy aims to advance consistently using existing administrative instruments.

item.title: “Não é hora da 6×1”, diz Paulo Skaf, presidente da Fiesp
item.description: Paulo Skaf, president of Fiesp, argues that the debate over ending the 6×1 work schedule and reducing the weekly workweek should be postponed until the next government cycle, emphasizing its complexity and inappropriateness during an election year. He warns that such a change could lower Brazil’s already low productivity and push more workers into informality, as seen in Chile after its 2024 reform. Skaf advocates for collective bargaining as the modern and appropriate approach, rather than top-down legislation.

item.title: Flávio vai ao Nordeste falar em Lula ultrapassado para crescer na região
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) pre-campaign believes his electoral growth hinges on reducing Lula's vote share in the Northeast while increasing his own support in the Southeast. The strategy centers on directly competing with the current president in key regions to determine the outcome of the 2026 election.
